strategic_profile.executive_summary: Dartmouth College is a private Ivy League research university known for its rigorous liberal arts curriculum, intimate undergraduate focus, and unique D-Plan quarter system. Set in rural New Hampshire, it fosters a tight-knit community with a strong emphasis on collaborative leadership, outdoor exploration, and global engagement.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Dartmouth recently reported a record-low 5.3% acceptance rate, heavily favoring Early Decision applicants who fill nearly half the class.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should apply ED if Dartmouth is their top choice, but must have competitive standardized test scores as testing requirements have been reinstated.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Dartmouth meets 100% of demonstrated need without loans and requires zero parent contribution for families earning under $125,000.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Despite a sticker price approaching $100,000, Dartmouth is highly affordable and actively accessible for low- and middle-income students.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The D-Plan academic calendar requires sophomore summer enrollment but allows immense flexibility for off-cycle internships.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students have a competitive advantage in securing highly sought-after internships during the fall or winter terms when students from other universities are taking traditional classes.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Campus life is heavily centered around Greek life (60% participation) and the Dartmouth Outing Club, shaped by its rural setting.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for a vibrant urban center may not be a good fit, whereas those seeking a deeply bonded, outdoor-oriented community will thrive.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant, 'work hard, play hard' culture with heavy participation in Greek life and outdoor activities.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Isolated rural setting; the Appalachian trail runs through campus, making outdoor recreation highly accessible but limiting immediate urban access.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: R1 research institution where undergraduates face minimal competition from graduate students, leading to extremely high undergraduate research participation.

current_status.test_policy_details: Standardized testing (SAT or ACT) is mandatory for the Class of 2029 and beyond. Scores are evaluated contextually based on the applicant's high school.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data derived from the 2024-2025 Common Data Set.

gpa.notes: Dartmouth does not officially report average GPA in the Common Data Set, though 95% of admits are in the top 10% of their high school class.

testing.policy_note: Reinstated mandatory testing for Class of 2029 and beyond.

colleges.name: Dartmouth College (Undergraduate)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: All undergraduate applicants apply generally to the college and declare their major in their sophomore year.
colleges.housing_profile: Residential college system with tight-knit communities.
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually curious, collaborative, outdoorsy, and socially engaged.

majors.name: Economics
majors.notes: Units represent Dartmouth's course count system (35 total courses required for graduation).

majors.name: Engineering Sciences
majors.special_requirements: B.E. requires an additional year (or intense scheduling) beyond the standard B.A.
majors.notes: Administered by the Thayer School of Engineering.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires one 100-word response (Why Dartmouth) and two 250-word responses chosen from distinct prompt lists exploring personal identity, curiosity, and societal impact.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Dartmouth heavily values collaborative, non-cutthroat students and gives structural advantages to rural/small-town applicants.

major_selection_tactics: Select smaller, non-impacted humanities or pure sciences to stand out.
Math is noted as a smaller but highly respected department that can differentiate STEM applicants.
Avoid claiming intended majors just for admission if the application profile does not support authentic interest.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Dartmouth does not admit by major. All students are admitted undeclared and can freely choose any major during their sophomore year.

scholarships.name: Dartmouth Need-Based Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Meets 100% of demonstrated need with $0 parent contribution for families under $125k.

ap_policy.general_rule: AP scores of 4 or 5 grant placement or exemption from introductory courses, but do NOT yield graduation credit.

ib_policy.general_rule: High level IB scores grant placement/exemption, but no graduation credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum of 17 credits can be transferred. Must be letter grade C or higher. Online courses are not accepted.

what_it_takes: Top 10% class rank and rigorous courseload.
Authentic passion for community and collaboration.
essay_tips: Showcase your authentic self, avoiding overly polished arrogance.
Highlight instances where you learned from failure or engaged with opposing viewpoints.
Demonstrate why the rural, close-knit environment fits your personality.
red_flags: Lack of interest in community or outdoor environments.
insights: The D-Plan makes social life fragmented but highly dynamic, as students are frequently coming and going from campus.
